openQuestions.question: Are schemas, handlers, docs, and catalog/playbook maintained from divergent sources of truth, and is there a generator or contract that would close most doc/schema-to-code drift?
openQuestions.answer: The common cause is divergent source-of-truth maintenance across public tool schemas, Zod schemas, handler forwarding, operator docs, and catalog/playbook surfaces. A generator or round-trip test boundary across those contracts would close the largest class.

openQuestions.question: Is metadata drift a generate-context / graph-metadata-backfill systemic defect or mostly isolated edits, and how many packets appear affected?
openQuestions.answer: Metadata drift is systemic. The graph metadata derivation reads frontmatter status and then falls back to implementation-summary/checklist completion, while current specs expose status in markdown metadata tables. A read-only 026 scan found 163 strong status contradictions.

openQuestions.question: Do entity-density cache staleness and atomic-save ordering corrupt retrieval or graph-channel routing under normal single-user operation?
openQuestions.answer: Entity-density staleness is a transient graph/degree routing correctness bug for memory_update, not durable data corruption. Atomic-save ordering is a durable consistency risk because DB indexing can commit before pending-file promotion.

openQuestions.question: Under the local single-user MCP threat model, should the two P0 scope/auth findings remain P0 or be recalibrated?
openQuestions.answer: Recalibrate to conditional P0: P0 for governed or multi-tenant isolation claims, P1 under the default trusted local stdio single-user model.

openQuestions.question: Did deep-loop fan-out reliability defects make prior review or research artifacts suspect?
openQuestions.answer: Prior artifacts are suspect at the orchestration/provenance layer, but not wholly invalid as review content. Use lineage reports and merged findings with caveats; do not trust summaries/attribution as release-gate proof.

resolvedQuestions.answer: Drift is caused by divergent source-of-truth maintenance across schemas, docs, handlers, and catalog/playbook surfaces.
Metadata drift is systemic. Status-source precedence ignores the spec template's visible metadata table and can mark draft/planned specs complete from implementation-summary/checklist evidence.
Entity-density staleness affects query routing freshness; atomic save ordering can create a durable DB/file mismatch on crash or promotion failure.
The scope/auth findings are real for governed scope but should be conditional P0 rather than unconditional P0 under trusted local stdio.
The weak layer is orchestration/provenance, not the existence of per-lineage review content.

keyFindings.title: Doc/schema drift is a contract-boundary problem, not one stale page
Graph metadata status drift is systemic and caused by status-source precedence
keyFindings.blastRadius: Read-only scan found 163 strong status contradictions under 026; exact count may shift with free-form status classification.

keyFindings.title: memory_update can stale graph-channel preservation until entity-density cache TTL expiry
keyFindings.blastRadius: Short entity-rich queries can over-route or under-route graph/degree until TTL expiry or an unrelated invalidation.

keyFindings.title: Atomic save commits index rows before final file promotion
keyFindings.blastRadius: Crash or promotion failure after DB commit can leave retrieval indexed to content not present at the canonical file path.

keyFindings.title: Scope/auth P0s are conditional on governed or multi-tenant isolation claims
keyFindings.blastRadius: Default local stdio: P1 correctness/security-hardening. Governed or multi-tenant mode: P0 isolation bypass.

keyFindings.title: Prior fan-out artifacts are content-usable but orchestration-suspect
keyFindings.blastRadius: Lineage reports and merged findings can inform remediation; orchestration summaries, executor provenance, concurrency claims, and iteration-cap compliance need rerun after fan-out fixes.

ruledOutDirections.approach: Treat drift as one stale operator doc page
ruledOutDirections.reason: The evidence spans schemas, docs, runtime forwarding, catalog/playbook assertions, and governance docs.

ruledOutDirections.approach: Treat metadata drift as isolated parent-pointer staleness
ruledOutDirections.reason: Strong status contradictions recur across many child packets and at least one 027 packet.

ruledOutDirections.approach: Treat entity-density staleness as durable data corruption
ruledOutDirections.reason: The cache feeds graph/degree routing decisions and has TTL/invalidation semantics; durable inconsistency comes from atomic-save ordering.

ruledOutDirections.approach: Dismiss scope/auth findings because stdio is trusted
ruledOutDirections.reason: Governed scope fields and fail-closed scope helpers create an isolation contract once callers supply scope.

ruledOutDirections.approach: Discard prior fan-out review content wholesale
ruledOutDirections.reason: Each review slice has five lineage reports, registries, logs, and completion sentinels; the weak layer is orchestration truth.
